mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of months when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premium and supports valuation multiples, driving positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 233.887 as of 2013-12
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods when both conditions are met, or correlation between (FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL) joint signal and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1000K units annualized rate signals robust residential construction demand and multiplier effects through employment and materials spending, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative returns in > 60% of 20-day windows following HOUST > 1000 threshold breach, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth acceleration above 2% annualized signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate capital deployment, which raises earnings expectations and drives positive 5-day forward returns.
regime_hint: cyclical expansion with improving manufacturing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or rolling 1-year Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerating simultaneously signals broad-based demand strength and consumer purchasing power, creating positive feedback loop that drives positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth > 3.5% annualized AND 3-month PAYEMS growth > 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 431879.0 and PAYEMS at 136877.0 as of 2013-12
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of dual-trigger instances, or correlation between joint signal and forward returns < 0.08 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate (increase > 0.50pp over 3 months) signals monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2013-12 indicates low starting point
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle initiated from low rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of tightening episodes, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising > 0.30pp in 3 months combined with declining payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month UNRATE increase > 0.30pp AND 3-month PAYEMS growth < 0.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% and PAYEMS at 136877.0 as of 2013-12
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of dual-deterioration instances, or joint signal correlation with forward returns > -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals weakening economic momentum that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of sub-2% growth quarters, or Rank IC > -0.03 (fails to show negative predictive power).
